LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY LORD'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. DISCOVERY MOTION PER SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 10(A)(1). PLEASE CALL AND CALENDAR MOTION IN DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-08-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY LORD'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. 1. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TO BE HELD NOT LATTER THAN DECEMBER 1, 2009, THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER CONCERNING AN EARLIER DATE FOR OUT-OF-STATE, OR INTERNATIONAL DEPOSITION 2. SANCTIONS SHALL ISSUE AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE SUM OF $500, PAYABLE WITHIN 45 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 3. PLAINTIFF APPEARED BY COUNSEL WELTIN LAW OFFICE, FORMAL SUBSTITUTION TO BE FILED THIS DATE. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Nielma C. Bassig, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Timothy Lord
Kevin P. McLaughlin, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Stephen Barry

After hearing, Defendant Timothy Lord's Motion to compel deposition and request for sanctions is granted.
1. Plaintiff's deposition to be held not latter than December 1, 2009, the parties shall meet and confer concerning an earlier date for out-of-state, or international deposition
2. Sanctions shall issue against plaintiff in the sum of $500, payable within 45 days of the date of this order.
3. Plaintiff appeared by counsel Weltin Law Office, formal substitution to be filed this date. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depostition And Request For Sanctions
